description: The final part of something; the point at which something ceases. 'Lumae' names the closing—where journeys conclude, where stories finish, where cycles complete.
sound_symbolism: The word comes to rest. 'Lu' flows toward completion, momentum slowing. 'Ma' grounds with finality, ending near. Finally 'e' opens to release, end arrived.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun with a possessor before it: 'nophi lumae' (the story's end). Opposite of 'thorui' (beginning). Endings in Phi are not negative—completion enables new beginnings.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: All things end—recognizing this brings appreciation for what is present and acceptance of change.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Endings were honored—the end of harvest, the end of life, transitions marked with ritual and respect.
